summary: Full backend + frontend testing of the new Alpha Terminal feature (public page + admin CRUD) plus regression on landing. All 20 backend pytest tests pass and all Playwright UI flows (nav, hero static timestamp, momentum table, coming-soon cards, admin login incl. wrong-password rejection, add/persist/delete round-trip, seed rows intact, drag handles present) succeed.

issues: Scanner select ([data-testid=admin-scanner-select]) renders with near-white background and white text -> option label 'Momentum Leaders' is nearly unreadable in the closed state. Give it explicit dark bg (e.g. bg-void / bg-[#0A0D18]) with white text, matching the rest of the form fields.

action_items: Fix contrast on /admin scanner select (white-on-white). Purely cosmetic; does not block functionality.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py Admin CRUD: update_stock does not preserve existing 'order' field explicitly (spread merge covers it, but StockCreate has no order field). Verified working, but consider adding an explicit reorder-safety unit test if update is called between reorders.
server.py: On unknown scanner in reorder_stocks the 'ordered_ids' updates silently no-op when ids don't match -- acceptable, but consider returning updated count for observability.
Admin.jsx save() runs sequential awaits inside a for-loop; fine for small lists but could be Promise.all for larger scanners in future.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral: TEST123 stock added and then deleted during UI test; TEST<uuid> added/deleted during backend test. Seed NVDA/CRWD/PLTR verified intact at end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Static timestamp verified as fixed string 'Today, 09:30 AM IST' (hard-coded in AlphaTerminal.jsx and returned by GET /api/terminal/scanners). Nav ordering (Research < Alpha Terminal < Insights) confirmed via bounding-rect x positions. Drag-drop reorder not automated in UI (HTML5 native DnD is hard to fire from Playwright) but backend reorder endpoint tested end-to-end and drag handle testids exist.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in feature scope.
